source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Ship Shoal 229 A Crane Structural Failure

event.top_event: Crane boom structural failure during load offloading
actions_taken: Crane operator called lead operator via company radio to alert of control failure
Lead operator met crane operator in cab to assess situation
Crane operator attempted to raise boom when it collapsed
Crane operator lowered load line block to enable deckhand to unhook slings
Vessel captain moved away from area
Company field mechanic brought to platform to secure crane boom in cradle
Third party crane inspector from South Marsh Island platform serviced crane on June 4, 2015
event.summary: On June 18, 2015 at approximately 1300 hours, the platform crane boom on Ship Shoal 229 A suffered major structural failure while offloading equipment onto an offshore vessel. The crane operator was lowering a load approximately 10-20 feet above the vessel deck when boom swing control became inactive. The operator heard what sounded like failure of boom hydraulic pistons and the boom and load began lowering uncontrollably. When the operator attempted to raise the boom, it collapsed in the middle, dropping the load onto the starboard side and deck of the vessel. The root cause was that the crane operator used an assumed load weight of 12,000-14,000 pounds rather than verifying the actual weight of 20,300 pounds, which exceeded the maximum rated capacity of 15,000 pounds at a 70-degree boom angle.
recommendations: Ensure cargo manifests show actual weights per individual lift, not total weights divided by number of lifts
Verify actual load weights prior to lifting using available weight indicators or load charts
Follow company policy requiring confirmation of actual load weight before crane operation
Consider installation of weight indicators in crane cabs to aid prevention of overload incidents
Communicate actual cargo weights from shore base to offshore facility and crane operator prior to lift preparation

hazards.name: Heavy load handling during offshore equipment transfer
Crane boom structural integrity under load

threats.name: Load weight exceeds crane rated capacity

consequences.name: Damage to vessel structure and equipment

controls.name: Cargo manifest weight labeling and communication
controls.barrier_role: Ensure accurate load weight information is available to crane operator
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent overload condition at source

supporting_text: The operator's shorebase cargo manifest dated 6/3/2015, shows the total (gross) weight of several loads rather than the actual weights of each individual load.
As shown on the manifest, the operators divided the 40,000-pound total weight by 3 to get the assumed (mistaken) weight of 13,300 pounds per lift.
The information on the cargo manifest dated 6/3/2015, should have indicated actual weights per lift and not total weights.

controls.name: Crane operator verification of actual load weight
controls.barrier_role: Operator confirms actual load weight against crane capacity before lifting
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring operator verification per API-RP-2D and company policy

supporting_text: The crane operator was using an assumed load weight of 12,000 - 14,000 pounds rather than verifying the actual weight of 20,300 pounds.
It is the crane operator's responsibility to confirm the actual weight of the load prior to lifting.

API-RP-2D 3.2.1 c states in part: 'The crane operator should verify that the hook load is within the crane's applicable Onboard or Offboard Rated Load at the radius at which the load is to be lifted.'

controls.name: Crane load chart review and lift planning
controls.barrier_role: Operator reviews load chart and plans lift considering equipment, weight, and distance
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control per company policy and API-RP-2D

supporting_text: Page 143 Crane Safety states: 'It is Ankor's policy that all crane operations, maintenance and inspections shall comply with API-RP-2D current edition as per the federal registry.'
Page 148 Lift Planning item 2 states: 'Always consider the following questions when planning a lift: Equipment? Weight? Distance?'
Page 155, item 1 states in part: 'The crane load chart shall be reviewed.'

controls.name: Crane operational limit enforcement
controls.barrier_role: Prevent operation beyond rated load limits
controls.lod_basis: Company policy requiring adherence to rated load limits

supporting_text: Page 149 Safe Crane Operation Guidelines item 6 states: 'No crane will be operated beyond its rated load limits. If in doubt do not make the lift.'
The maximum rated capacity for this crane at a boom angle of 70 degrees was 15,000 pounds. The actual weight of the load greatly exceeded the lifting capacity of the boom.

controls.name: Crane weight indicator installation
controls.barrier_role: Provide real-time load weight measurement to prevent overload
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to detect actual load weight in real-time

supporting_text: Although not required by RP-2D, a weight indicator in the crane may have aided in the prevention of this accident.

There is a larger Sea-King crane located on this structure with a weight indicator installed. If the operator had instructed the motor vessel to move over to the C structure upon realization of lifting difficulties, they might have discovered the true weights of the loads.

controls.name: Hydraulic system maintenance and inspection
controls.barrier_role: Maintain hydraulic system integrity and prevent piston failure
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to maintain crane structural integrity

supporting_text: The crane inspector noted on his report that he increased the hydraulic relief pressure from 2000 psi to 2500 psi.
Both men noticed what sounded to them like failure of the boom hydraulic pistons and then realized that the boom and load were lowering uncontrollably.

controls.name: Boom control system monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect loss of boom swing control and alert operator
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to detect control system failure

supporting_text: While lowering the load down to the vessel, the crane operator realized something was wrong when the booms swing control became inactive.
The crane operator called his lead operator via company radio alerting him of the situation.

controls.name: Emergency boom lowering procedure
controls.barrier_role: Enable controlled lowering of load in case of control failure
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to mitigate consequences of control loss

supporting_text: The boom and load were lowering uncontrollably. The crane operator then tried to raise the boom when it collapsed in the middle dropping the load onto the starboard side and deck of the vessel.

controls.name: Load securing and deckhand procedures
controls.barrier_role: Safely unhook load and move vessel away from danger zone
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to mitigate consequences after load drop

supporting_text: The crane operator then lowered the load line block to enable the deckhand to unhook the slings, which allowed the vessel's captain to move away from the area.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
